snippet: Schools are required to conduct regular maintenance of their school grounds following the asset management roles and responsibilities framework and their school maintenance plan (where applicable). When a school has arranged for an arborist to conduct a routine tree assessment and there are trees classified as high-risk, the school may apply to the Safe Tree Program (the program) for financial support for high-risk tree works and the cost of the arborist report. The arborist’s report, quote for the high-risk tree works and proof of payment for the arborist report must be included in the application. If the application is approved, the cost of the arborist report and the high-risk tree works will be reimbursed. Schools must not commence any works until they are informed that their application has been successful. If the application is unsuccessful (no high-risk trees or works completed before assessment), schools will incur the cost of the arborist report and any tree works planned or completed. In cases where schools do not have the capacity to arrange an arborist assessment and undertake works through a qualified arborist, the program can provide advice and financial support to assess, manage, and have high-risk tree works conducted. In this situation, the Victorian School Building Authority (VSBA) will lead the assessment of trees on the school grounds and the implementation of any high-risk tree works. The program is managed by the VSBA Response Programs Unit, who can be contacted via email: vsba.response.program@education.vic.gov.au
